explanation_md: The friend `operator+` accesses `m_vol` directly (private access), sums 10+25=35, and returns `Box{35}`. `z.vol()` returns 35.

prompt_md: Why do arithmetic operators like `operator+` use `const T&` parameters rather than `T` parameters?

options.a: Because `operator+` can only accept `const` arguments at the call site
options.b: To avoid unnecessary copies of each operand on every call to the operator
options.c: Because non-const references would allow callers to pass rvalue expressions
options.d: The compiler requires `const` references specifically for overloaded operators

explanation_md: Taking by `const T&` passes a reference to the existing object with no copy. Taking by value `T` would make a full copy of each operand every time the operator is called, which is wasteful. `const` ensures the function promises not to modify its arguments.

prompt_md: A class `Color` has private fields `r`, `g`, `b`. Which declaration inside the class correctly grants `operator+` private access?

options.a: `Color operator+(const Color& a, const Color& b);`
options.b: `friend Color operator+(const Color& a, const Color& b);`
options.c: `static Color operator+(const Color& a, const Color& b);`
options.d: `public: Color operator+(const Color& a, const Color& b);`

explanation_md: The `friend` keyword in a class declaration grants the named external function access to private and protected members. Option (a) without `friend` is just a member function declaration (wrong signature for a binary op). `static` and `public` do not confer friend access.
`a + b + c` is `(a + b) + c` = `N{7} + N{5}` = `N{12}`. Each call to `operator+` returns a new object by value, enabling chaining.

options.a: `friend` functions cannot take non-const reference parameters
options.b: The operator modifies `a` in-place and returns a reference to it, so `s1` is mutated and `s3` is an alias for `s1` — `a + b` should return a new object by value, not modify the left operand
options.c: The `Score` class needs a default constructor for the operator to work
options.d: Binary operators can only be defined as member functions, not friend functions

explanation_md: Arithmetic `operator+` must return a *new* value, not mutate an operand. This implementation changes `s1` and returns a reference to it, so after the call `s1.pts()` is 30 and `s3` is just another name for `s1`. The fix: `return Score{a.m_pts + b.m_pts};` with a by-value return and `const Score&` for the first parameter.
